_metadata.source: photonics.pdf - Optical Materials Express Vol. 15, No. 12, 1 Dec 2025
_metadata.title: Design and experimental validation of a high-efficiency multi-zone metasurface waveguide in-coupler
_metadata.authors: Pei Xiong, Jeremy Goodsell, Daniel Nikolov, Jannick P. Rolland, Nick A. Vamivakas
_metadata.extraction_notes: All values extracted directly from paper text, Table 1, Fig 2(e) (multimodal read p.3), and Fig 3(a-f) (multimodal read p.5)

system_parameters.grating_period_note: Derived from system specification that smallest diffraction angle for field (-10 deg, 0 deg) is just larger than the critical angle of N-BK7

refractive_indices.note: Exact numerical values not explicitly stated in paper text; standard catalog values given here for reference

TiO2_at_532nm.paper_description: high refractive index, low absorption in visible spectrum; ALD deposition yields higher index than sputtered TiO2

N_BK7_at_532nm.note: Inferred from critical angle of ~41.2 deg (paper states lower TIR bound is 41.2-41.5 deg)

critical_angle_N_BK7.source: Fig 3 caption states reflection angle range starts at 41.2 deg; text states incident angle in glass starts at 41.5 deg (just above critical angle); text: 'the smallest diffraction angle for the field (-10 deg, 0 deg) is just larger than the critical angle of N-BK7'
critical_angle_N_BK7.note: critical angle = arcsin(n_air / n_BK7); consistent with 41.2 deg lower bound for TIR propagation

grating_equation.form: n_d * sin(theta_d) = n_i * sin(theta_i) + m * lambda / d
grating_equation.usage_in_paper: Used to convert XFOV incident angles (-10 to +10 deg) to first-order diffraction angles in glass (41.5 to 62.5 deg); simulation source placed inside glass substrate

MFE.definition: Minimum Field Efficiency - minimum in-coupling efficiency for all fields within FOV; upper limit of brightness for achieving uniform output since low-efficiency areas cannot be digitally brightened

design_parameters_for_theoretical_limit.assumption: pupil and in-coupler equally sized and co-planar

fig2e_per_zone_targets.note: Ideal targets assuming sum of two interaction efficiencies = 100% (from Ref [27] theoretical framework). Values read from Fig 2(e) multimodal image.
fig2e_per_zone_targets.layout: Zone1 | Zone2 | Zone3 arranged laterally; sizes are widths along XFOV direction

unit_cell_structure.description: One nano-beam + one nano-pillar per unit cell
unit_cell_structure.nano_beam: runs along x-direction (perpendicular to grating vector); its width is in the x-direction
unit_cell_structure.height_note: All three zones share the same height; constraint imposed to accommodate fabrication
unit_cell_structure.zones_2_3_note: Nano-pillars in zones 2 and 3 display partial circular shapes; pillar dimensions exceed unit cell boundaries during optimization; sharp corners appear slightly rounded after fabrication
unit_cell_structure.meta_atom_material: TiO2 deposited by ALD (Atomic Layer Deposition)

per_zone_geometry.note: Designed values from Table 1 and Fig 3(a-c) labels. Measured values from Table 1 (SEM, within +/-20 nm). Height=250 nm for all zones.

zone2.pillar_type: partial circular (extends beyond unit cell boundary)

zone3.y_period_source: Fig 3(c) label on 3D geometry diagram (reading uncertain; may represent separation or partial dimension)
zone3.pillar_type: partial circular (extends beyond unit cell boundary)

fig3df_efficiency_curves_approximate.note: Values APPROXIMATED from graphical reading of Fig 3(d-f) plots. Red curves = 1st-order diffraction efficiency vs incident angle (-10 to +10 deg). Blue curves = 0th-order reflection efficiency vs secondary (internal) incident angle (41.2 to 62.5 deg). Black curves = sum. Reading precision +/-3 to 5%.
fig3df_efficiency_curves_approximate.angle_correspondence: Primary incident angles (-10, -5, 0, +5, +10 deg) correspond to secondary (internal) angles approximately (41.5, 46.5, 52.0, 57.0, 62.5 deg) in N-BK7

zone1_fig3d.description: Zone 1: non-uniform, high at -10 deg, gradually decreasing toward +10 deg (limited angular bandwidth). Optimization merit balances efficiencies at both ends of XFOV.

zone2_fig3e.description: Zone 2: much flatter efficiency curve than zone 1; inherently broader angular bandwidth. Optimized only for -10 deg field.

zone3_fig3f.description: Zone 3: flat efficiency curve with low diffraction and high reflection; broader angular bandwidth; trade-off between peak efficiency and angular bandwidth.

simulation_parameters.optimizer: Multi-Variable Optimization and Scanning Tool (MOST) in RSoft

fill_factor_duty_cycle.note: Not explicitly stated as fill factor in paper; derived from bar width / x-period
fill_factor_duty_cycle.ranges_mentioned: No explicit fill factor or duty cycle range given; optimization variables are beam width, pillar diameter, pillar height, beam-pillar separation, and y unit cell size

_reproduction_note.purpose: Input targets for S4 RCWA reproduction in current project
